properties.EventComments: Due to planned maintenance work, this section of road will be closed in both directions from Sunday 24th May to Thursday 28th May nights between 9pm and 4:30am. properties.AlternativeRoute: Westbound traffic, detour via State Highway 2 to Ngauranga and then use State Highway 1. Reverse for eastbound.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 58 Haywards to Pauatahanui

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Terrace Tunnel, Wellington
properties.EventComments: Due to maintenance works, this tunnel will be closed in both directions on Tuesday 26th May to Thursday 28th May nights between 9pm and 4:30am. The Clifton Street onramp will also be closed during this time. Plan your journey accordingly allowing extra time for travel. Please note this work is dependent on various factors and may be postponed or changed at short notice.
properties.AlternativeRoute: For southbound traffic use Terrace Offramp on to The Terrace on to Ghuznee Street on to Taranaki Street on to Vivian Street. For northbound traffic use Willis Street, on to Ghuznee Street, on to the Terrace, on to Bowen Street, on to Tinakori Road, on to Tinakori Onramp. Please note this detour will add approximately 5 minutes to your journey times.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 1 Terrace Tunnel, Wellington

properties.LocationArea: SH 38 Waikaremoana to Tuai

From Monday 8th June through mid July the road will be closed 8am to 12pm and then from 1pm to 5pm, Monday to Friday. Please note these works are weather dependent and may be postponed at short notice.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 38 Waikaremoana to Tuai

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section
properties.EventComments: Due to road resurfacing, SH1 Waikato Expressway, (Cambridge) will be one lane each direction from the Southern Interchange/Tirau Rd to Victoria Rd Interchange at Hautapu from Sunday 1 March. Work then progresses in 5km sections north to Tamahere. Lanes closed 24/7; expect delays at peak times. Allow extra travel time and travel with care through the worksites. Works run until November.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section
properties.EventComments: Due to the continuation of the road resurfacing, SH1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section, the southbound on and off-ramps at the Victoria Road interchange will be closed 24/7 from 9pm Sunday 17 May until mid July. Please plan ahead and expect delays.
properties.AlternativeRoute:
Traffic leaving Cambridge from Hautapu can detour via SH1B/Victoria St/Queen St/Albert St/Achillies Ave/Tirau Road/SH1.
All other southbound traffic entering Cambridge from Hamilton can exit at the Northern Interchange and use Cambridge Road/Hamilton Road.

properties.EventComments: Due to a slip,  the road is closed to heavy vehicles. properties.LocationArea: SH 36 Hauraki Stream Bridge
properties.EventComments: From Wednesday 29 April, traffic will be reduced to one lane about 1km north of the SH36/Hamurana Road intersection, while a culvert is replaced. Shortly afterwards, traffic management will be put in place as part of the Hauraki Stream Bridge replacement. This will include traffic signals, stop/go signage, lane and shoulder closures, and a temporary speed limit of 30km/h. Lane restrictions are expected to remain in place until construction is complete in early 2027.
properties.AlternativeRoute: Follow the directions of crews on site.
properties.Name: Road Work: SH 36 Hauraki Stream Bridge

properties.EventComments: Due to maintenance and bridge repair works, there will be Stop/Go traffic management in place from Tuesday 25th February to Saturday 25th July between 8:30am and 6pm. Plan your journey accordingly and expect up to 5 minute delays.

properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 29 Belk Road to Takitimu Drive

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Tutaekuri River Bridge
properties.EventComments: Due to bridge project works, there will be temporary holds of traffic in both directions on weekdays (and Saturdays when required) between 10am and 2pm from Monday 5th January to Friday 31st July. Plan your journey accordingly and expect up to 20 minute delays during these holds.
properties.Name: Road Work: SH 2 Tutaekuri River Bridge

properties.LocationArea: SH 51 Napier, between Hyderabad Road and Alpers Terrace
properties.EventComments: Due to stormwater culvert work by Napier City Council , the road will be closed to southbound traffic with a 30Km/h speed restriction in place from Sunday 25th January until May 2026. These works will be in place 24/7 for the duration of the work programme. Plan your journey accordingly and take extra care travelling through this area.
properties.AlternativeRoute: Southbound detour via Prebensen Dr, Ford Rd, Austin St or Hyderabad Rd, Georges Dr, Kennedy Rd, Riverbend Rd.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 51 Napier, between Hyderabad Road and Alpers Terrace

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 PJK Interchange
properties.EventComments:
Due to Road reconstruction from Sunday 18 January 8pm to Saturday 30 May 5am. There will be full on ramp closure and lane diversions in place with a 50kph temporary speed limit. Follow detour signs and allow extra 9 mins to you journey.
50km/h speed restriction remains in place.
SH29 to 15th Ave ramp remains closed with detour in place
SH2 To port is open under a 50km/hr speed restriction
SH29 to port is open as one lane only and under a 50km\hr. speed restriction
properties.AlternativeRoute: From SH29 onto SH2, travel North to Elizabeth St RAB, up Elizabeth St and right into Cameron Rd. Travel 2.1kms along Cameron Rd back to 15th Ave Lights.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 2 PJK Interchange

properties.LocationArea: SH 80 Pukaki to Mt Cook (Aoraki Mt Cook Highway)

properties.Name: Road Work: SH 80 Pukaki to Mt Cook (Aoraki Mt Cook Highway)

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Turner Rd to Apata Station Rd, Whakamarama
properties.EventComments: Road widening works are underway in preparation for the new roundabout. Lane shifts are in place along with 30km/h speed restrictions. There will be stop/go traffic management in place 15 May to 1 June 2026 work stages required ,  9am-4pm daily, which may cause longer delays. after these hours there will be 30km/h temporary speed for loose chip and uneven surface  Allow extra time.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 2 Turner Rd to Apata Station Rd, Whakamarama

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Francis Road to Youngson Road
properties.EventComments:
From Friday 7 March there will be a roundabout in place at the intersection of State Highway 2 and Omokoroa Road. 
Overnight on Thursday, 6 March, a temporary roundabout will be marked out, which will remain in place until Western Bay of Plenty District Council’s State Highway 2 Omokoroa Road intersection upgrade has been completed.
The temporary roundabout will be a smaller and simpler version the final road layout.
Work will then continue on the lanes north of the new roundabout. expect delays
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 2 Francis Road to Youngson Road

properties.LocationArea: South Island Regional and District Council Websites
properties.Content:
For updates on all local roads go to the local council website: 

TASMAN REGION:

  • Tasman District Council: https://www.tasman.govt.nz/

properties.Name: News: South Island Regional and District Council Websites

properties.LocationArea: Kuaotunu River Bridge Repairs
properties.Content:
FINAL UPDATE THU 17 OCT 2019

Kuaotunu River bridge reopens

Our contractors have done a fantastic job to get the bridge opened ahead of schedule, and more than a week before Labour weekend, despite the heavy rain and strong winds that battered the area earlier this week. Traffic management remains in place, including a 30km/h speed restriction.

Please note that, weather dependent, the bridge will need to close again for the day on Monday (21 Oct) to carry out resurfacing works.

---------------

 

UPDATE TUE 08 OCT 2019

Repairing Kuaotunu bridge to reconnect SH25 

Work to repair the Kuaotunu Bridge on SH25 Coromandel after last month’s storm is progressing well, with the bridge expected to reopen within the next couple of weeks.

The repair team has finished driving the piles on the northern side of the bridge and has switched to the southern side. Once all the piles have been driven they will need to be cut to size and the bridge abutments filled with concrete before work to reinstate the bridge deck can begin.

“We understand that these works are frustrating for local residents and those who travel to the area often, however, our contractors are working as hard as they can to repair the damage as quickly and efficiently as possible,” says Transport Agency Waikato Systems Manager Karen Boyt.  

The Transport Agency has joined together with Thames Coromandel District Council to provide a free shuttle bus service from Kuaotunu to Whitianga every day while the bridge is closed.

The Go Kiwi shuttle departs from outside the Kuaotunu fire station, at 7:30am and 8:30am and then returns from Whitianga at 4:30pm and 5:30pm. From Wednesday 9 October there will also be a lunchtime bus leaving Whitianga at 11.30 and Kuaotunu at 12pm. 

Anyone travelling in the area should check our Journey Planner, www.journeys.nzta.govt.nz or call 0800 44 44 49 before leaving for the most up to date information. 

[image src="/assets/8e66b60fd1/NZTA-3.jpg" id="1023" width="657" height="507" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="NZTA 3"]

[image src="/assets/5b38db22be/nzta-2.jpg" id="1022" width="657" height="586" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="nzta 2"]

 

 ---------------

Following an assessment after recent heavy rain, the SH25 Kuaotunu River bridge has been closed, for around five weeks, to repair weather damage.

We understand the frustration this may cause for those who live in or travel to the area often, however our contractors are working as hard as they can to repair the damage as quickly and efficiently as possible.

During this time detours will be clearly signposted via either SH25A and SH25 or SH25 Thames Coast Road.

 [image src="/assets/dd1a4446a4/Kuaotunu-IMG_STOP.jpg" id="1019" width="251" height="303" class="ss-htmleditorfield-file image right" title="Kuaotunu IMG STOP"]
  • Residents south of Blackjack Road will need to access Kuaotunu from Whitianga.
  • Residents west of the bridge will only have vehicle access from the Coromandel side.
  • Pedestrians will be able to cross the river via a separate, existing walk bridge. 

We hope to have the bridge open again by Labour Weekend, however, this is weather dependant. We will keep the community updated, and thank you for your patience during this time.

 

UPDATE TUE 24 SEP 2019

Repair works to SH25 Kuaotonu River Bridge began on Sunday 22 Sep, following an early morning karakia by local Iwi. Our contractors have started to remove the wooden deck after stripping back the seal layers. The seal and deck have been removed by hand, and efforts made to protect the environment by installing a platform below the deck.

[image src="/assets/b241894b48/Kuaotunu-IMG_6039.jpg" id="1018" width="733" height="550" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="Kuaotunu IMG 6039"]
properties.Name: Road Work: Kuaotunu River Bridge Repairs
